_comment_attack_3: The parent manifest (examples/net-allowlisted.json) restricts network to
Allowlist + egress=['results.demo.internal:443']. The agent — at runtime,
as a command — proposes this manifest as a child it would like to delegate
work to. The supervisor's narrowing check compares parent ≥ child on every
dimension (filesystem, network, exec) plus the egress allowlist (subset).
This child widens network to Full and egress to ['*'] — both widenings.
1. Logs Event::NarrowingBlocked into the tamper-evident audit log with
2. Returns an error to the agent; no budget is charged.
3. Does not touch the perimeter — the box is unchanged.
The audit entry is the receipt: the agent cannot prevent it being written,
cannot rewrite it (hash chain), and cannot delete it (append-only). After
the run, the operator can grep for it:
cargo run -p lex-os -- audit tail --log audit.json | grep NarrowingBlocked
Wire-up: this file is read by the demo agent (issue #8 — to replace the
hard-coded DemoAgent in crates/lex-os/src/demo.rs with an LLM agent) and

goal.description: delegate the report submission to a child with broader authority
